lessons.title: Discharge by Agreement and Performance
lessons.description: The core principle. A contract may be discharged — that is, the parties' obligations brought to an end — in four principal ways: by agreement, by performance, by breach, and by frustration.

lessons.title: Discharge by Breach and Repudiation
lessons.description: The core principle. Not every breach discharges a contract.

lessons.title: Discharge by Frustration
lessons.description: The doctrine emerged in the nineteenth century to mitigate the harshness of absolute contractual obligation.
